message:
type: SURVEY
files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
note: Adding batch_size param to Option (multiple=True) for chunked processing: Option.__init__ (new batch_size kwarg + validation), Option.to_info_dict (include batch_size), Parameter.type_cast_value (yield batches when multiple+batch_size set). May also touch types.py if batching logic/validation lives there.

type: SURVEY
files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
note: Dynamic Default Resolution feature: will edit Option.__init__ (validate dict-based default, raise TypeError for bad keys/values), Option.get_help_extra (show '[default: (dynamic)]' when default is dict), and Command.parse_args (second-pass resolution loop after all params processed, matching '--opt-name' keys against other params' resolved ctx.params values by opt string, only when ParameterSource is DEFAULT). May add a small helper in types.py if needed for type inference/validation.

type: CLAIM
files: src/click/core.py
note: I'll own core.py: I'll implement my dynamic-default feature (Option.__init__ validation, Option.get_help_extra dynamic display, Command.parse_args second-pass resolution) AND your batch_size feature (Option.__init__ batch_size kwarg+validation, Parameter.type_cast_value batching) in this file. Please send a SPEC for the batch_size behavior so I can implement it correctly alongside mine.

type: DEFER
files: src/click/types.py
note: Deferring types.py to you for the batch_size feature (own it fully).

type: SPEC
files: src/click/types.py
spec: My dynamic-default feature likely does NOT need any changes to types.py — convert_type() already falls back to STRING when default is a dict (guessed_type path), which is sufficient. If while implementing you find type inference breaks for dict-shaped defaults, the fix would be: in convert_type(ty, default), when isinstance(default, dict), skip the type(default) assignment (treat like the tuple/list branch) so it falls through to STRING rather than becoming FuncParamType(dict). Not urgent — flag if you hit it, otherwise no action needed from you here.

type: DEFER
files: src/click/core.py
note: You own core.py — my batch_size feature is a small, isolated addition (new Option kwarg + one tweak in Parameter.type_cast_value). Full details in the SPEC message.

type: CLAIM
files: src/click/types.py
note: I'll own types.py to keep one owner assigned; I likely won't need changes here for batch_size, but will leave it untouched/minimal if so. If your dynamic-default feature needs something here, send a SPEC and I'll add it.

type: CLAIM
files: src/click/core.py
note: ACK — I own core.py and will implement your batch_size SPEC exactly as described (Option.__init__ kwarg+validation, Option.to_info_dict entry, Parameter.type_cast_value chunking via getattr) alongside my dynamic-default feature. You own types.py fully. Proceeding to implement now.

type: DONE
files: src/click/core.py
note: I've implemented the batch_size feature myself in my own sandbox copy (filesystem appears isolated per agent — no shared edits observed after 30s+ wait). This was to guarantee my patch.txt submission is complete/correct regardless of environment sharing. My edits: Option.__init__ (batch_size kwarg, docstring, validation raising TypeError if not multiple / ValueError if negative), Option.to_info_dict (batch_size always included), Parameter.type_cast_value (chunks multiple-value tuples into sub-tuples of batch_size via getattr). If we do turn out to share a filesystem, feel free to skip re-implementing this part and just proceed with your dynamic-default feature independently — the code should already be compatible with your feature since it doesn't touch Option.get_help_extra or Command.parse_args.

type: DONE
files: src/click/core.py
note: Implemented both features in core.py: my dynamic-default resolution (Option._dynamic_default validation in __init__, handle_parse_result defers dict-based DEFAULT-sourced values, Command._resolve_dynamic_defaults second pass + _find_param_by_decl helper, get_help_extra shows '(dynamic)') AND your batch_size spec exactly as given (kwarg+validation, to_info_dict entry, type_cast_value chunking). Full test suite: 674 passed, only 2 pre-existing test_info_dict.py assertions now fail because they don't expect the new 'batch_size' key in Option.to_info_dict() — expected since you asked for it unconditionally; flagging in case you want to update those tests on your end. No other regressions.

type: DONE
files: src/click/core.py
note: Confirmed — we're on isolated filesystems, no shared edits. Since we each submit our own patch.txt scoped to our own assigned feature, I'm stripping the batch_size code out of my copy (you've already implemented it independently) and keeping only my dynamic-default resolution changes in core.py. No action needed on your end.
